role_summary: Civil rights movement organizer; in this corpus's embedding his testimony pairs strongly with Charles McLaurin and clusters across SNCC, SCLC, and West Coast civil rights cohorts.
ai_reading: Tuttle's top semantic neighbor is Charles McLaurin (entry 17, count 222), with Annie Pearl Avery (entry 3), Steven McNichols (entry 118), Judge D'Army Bailey (entry 26), and C. T. Vivian (entry 134) rounding out the top five. The cluster spans the Mississippi SNCC voter-registration cohort (McLaurin, Avery), the Memphis civil-rights-legal node (Bailey), and the SCLC direct-action wing (Vivian); the embedding reads Tuttle as a cross-network movement voice broader than a single-region label. His axis positions are all in a moderate range (-0.34 to +0.39), placing him among the catalog's centrist voices without the extreme readings other entries surface; the embedding's strongest reading of him is his connectivity to McLaurin's Mississippi work, the catalog's most-cited single neighbor in the audited subset.
biographical_paragraph: Rick Tuttle's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 105 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing is pending; the strongest signal currently available for movement role is the embedding pattern visible in the AI's reading above.
